summary: The test suite covers all five key requirement areas — max-age, keep-latest-N, max-total-size, combined policies, dry-run, and error handling — earning a solid coverage score. The keep-latest independence test (two separate run groups) and the 'no deletion when at limit' boundary test are notably good. Design is clean: a write_fixture helper eliminates repetition, setup/teardown gives proper isolation, a fixed REFDATE makes age calculations deterministic, and test names clearly describe intended behavior. Rigor is the main weak point: the max-total-size tests only exercise deleting exactly one artifact — they never verify the greedy multi-artifact deletion path where more than one item must be removed to satisfy the limit. Other missing edge cases include an empty CSV (zero data rows), keep-latest where N >= group size (nothing should be deleted), all artifacts exceeding max-age (total deletion), invalid/non-numeric argument values, and any test combining all three policies simultaneously. The error tests also only check for 'ERROR' substring presence rather than specific messages. The two-layer approach (bats unit tests + act integration tests with exact value assertions) is architecturally strong, but the unit test layer needs richer boundary coverage to fully trust the suite against regressions.
